entry.title: Re: G/L account 1001 cannot be used (please correct)Messa...
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you follow below steps

1.Identify Field Status Group: Use T-code FSOO to identify the Field Status group for the GL account.
2.Verify Field Status Group Configuration: In T-code OBC4, verify and decide if fields should be Suppress, Required, or Optional Entry as required .
3.Adjust Cost Center Settings: In T-code OME9, adjust settings to define if the cost center should be Mandatory, Optional, Display, or Hidden, based on business needs.
4.Ensure Consistency: Ensure configurations in both T-codes are consistent for seamless GL postings.

Thanks,

Rajesh Reddy

entry.title: Re: FS Items and GL Account Mapping Table into SAC
entry.content:
@sachinagarwal_680 

GL Accounts to FS Items mapping is done in SAP GR. You can import the FS Items from GR into SAC dimension using the Odata service API_GRMASTERDATA_SRV. I am not sure if you need the GL Account also, if yes you would need another dimension for that and use the OData service API_GLACCOUNTINCHARTOFACCOUNTS_SRV. The mapping between GL to FS Item will not be available in SAC unless you then create an attribute in GL Account dimension in SAC and map FS Items manually as you uploaded in GR.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Replication between Tenants
entry.content:
Hello Specs !

I have two tenants for Datasphere a Production and Test Systems.

I've been asked to migrate data from Prod To TEST for some testing purpose so I've created a space and mapped the Prod System from Test (also added the system to whitelist).
All in all I've a successfuly connection created with an Analysis user ready to use replication flow. 

My Problem is that when I try to run a replication flow the replication flow run on error.

The error messages like this :  insufficient privilege or this "Source setup failed due to the following error: Failed to prepare source replication artifacts due to database error. SAP DBTech JDBC: [258]: insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid '40F6D4EB4460104A957A47ED58309D7E'. Please refer to SAP Note 2980489 to know which privilege is missing for the current user. Contact the system administrator for the HANA system to address the issue and then resume the replication task. CorrelationId: fa46e992-1f9a-4634-1900-daa69d271c86@26-Jan-2025 06:59:49."
So think I have issue with the user.

I've used call SYS.GET_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE_ERROR_DETAILS (<guid>, ?) but I couldn't find any error message there. And although I am system owner I cannot seems to see any option to grant higher rights for analysis user (at least I cannot find) so I am clueles how to grant the necessary rights or the necessary users for migrate data from one tenant to another)

Could you please help me out how to create user that has necessary right or grant him the necessary rights.

entry.title: Live Session on SAP Next Generation Aftermarket Cloud!
entry.content:
Who Should Attend? 
This session is ideal for automotive consultants, industry experts, and professionals looking to enhance their knowledge and stay updated on the latest trends and technologies in the automotive aftermarket.
 
Why Attend?
The experts will highlight the importance of technological disruption, environmental changes, and rising competitiveness as drivers for transformation. They will cover trends and strategies such as optimized operations, sustainable logistics, and the integration of network-aware applications. The goal is to enable aftersales to become an intelligent, sustainable enterprise.
 
Date: 2025-02-19 Wednesday
Time: 10:00 - 11:00 am CET (GMT+1)
Speakers: Frank Globisch, Markus Kraemer
Register now: https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/next-generation-aftermarket-cloud
 
What to Expect:
  • Automotive Strategic Priorities: Learn about the key strategic priorities driving the automotive industry, including servitization, customer experience, software-defined vehicles, sustainability, and resilient supply chains.
  • Trends & Strategies: Discover the key drivers of transformation in the aftermarket industry, including technological disruption, environmental changes, and rising competitiveness.
  • Fulfillment & Execution: Learn about the highlights in fulfillment and execution, and how SAP's Aftermarket Cloud can optimize your operations.
  • Aftermarket Planning: Gain insights into advanced planning techniques and how AI and machine learning can enhance your planning processes.
  • Use Cases: Explore real-world use cases and success stories from leading automotive com
The presentation focuses on the Next Generation Aftermarket Cloud by SAP, highlighting its strategic priorities and innovative solutions for the automotive industry. Key areas covered include:
  1. Automotive Strategic Priorities:
    • Servitization: Transitioning from product-based to service-based business models.
    • Customer Experience: Enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.
    • Software-Defined Vehicles: Integrating software to improve vehicle functionality.
    • Sustainability: Focusing on sustainable business practices.
    • Resilient Supply Chain and Smart Manufacturing: Creating flexible and efficient supply chains.
  2. Focus on Aftermarket – Customer Support:
    • Customer Centricity: Providing a 720-degree view of the vehicle and customer.
    • Connected Vehicle: Leveraging telematics and vehicle data.
    • Predictive & Proactive Customer Care: Using AI for predictive analysis and customer interaction.
    • Circular Economy: Enabling automated decision-making for parts exchange and second-life parts.
  3. Reinvent Aftersales:
    • Next Gen. Aftermarket Parts Management: Capturing and managing demand across the OEM network.
    • AI Optimized Forecasting: Using AI algorithms for inventory planning and real-time execution.
    • Flexible Global Network Structure: Adapting to evolving network structures and optimizing inventory.
  4. Ever-modern SAP Aftermarket System:
    • AI Factory: Utilizing AI and machine learning for long-term forecasting and automation.
    • Generative AI Hub: Leveraging generative AI for innovative solutions.
    • Quantum Computing for Logistics: Exploring quantum computing for logistics optimization.
How to Register?
Register now to secure your spot for this informative and engaging live session. Next Generation Aftermarket Cloud
 
Don't miss this opportunity to learn from industry leaders and gain valuable insights into the future of the automotive aftermarket. 
 
We look forward to seeing you there!

Grzegorz Posyniak
Global Learning Portfolio Manager for Industries,
SAP Product and Solution Learning, SIX Solution and Innovation Experience

entry.title: Process to Convert a Purchase Order to an inbound delivery with batch split
entry.content:
Purchase Order:
A purchase order in SAP is a formal request issued to a supplier for the delivery of goods or services. It includes detailed information such as item descriptions, quantities, and delivery terms. SAP helps track the status of purchase orders, ensuring timely procurement and accurate inventory management. 

 

Inbound Delivery:
An inbound delivery in SAP refers to the receipt of goods from a supplier or vendor. It contains details like shipping dates, transportation information, and the items being delivered. SAP enables efficient management of inbound deliveries, ensuring smooth goods receipt and accurate updates to inventory levels.

 

Batch:
A batch in SAP is a unique identifier assigned to a group of products produced or received together under the same conditions. Batches are used to track products for quality control, traceability, and compliance purposes. SAP allows the management and monitoring of batch attributes like expiry dates, production dates, and specific characteristics.

 

Batch Split:
Batch split in SAP refers to the process of dividing a batch into smaller quantities based on specific requirements such as shipping or storage needs. It is commonly used when products need to be allocated to different sales orders or storage locations. SAP facilitates batch splitting to optimize inventory management and ensure accurate tracking.

 

Steps to Convert a Purchase Order to an inbound delivery with batch split

Step1:

Create an Inbound delivery using the transaction code VL31N

 

Enter the Purchase Order details and the delivery date:

 

 

Step2:

Select the line item.

Enter the delivery quantity and vendor batch number.

Then save it.

 

Step3:

If you need to create multiple batches for the same vendor batch

Select the line item and click on the click on the batch split button.

 

Step4:

Considering you need product from multiple batches.

Provide the quantity and the supplier batch details

Then select all the line items and click on the batch creation button.

 

Step5:

Then provide the date of manufacturing and city of origin for all the new batches created.

Then click on the enter key.

 

Step6:

Then save, the inbound delivery will be created.

 

Conclusion:

Steps to Convert a Purchase Order to an inbound delivery with batch split is provided.

entry.title: Re: How to hide the page Tab in Build apps Preview page
entry.content:
I believe the way you do this is by creating custom navigation (i.e., the default navigation does not support this).

Here is a blog by @marihirvi: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/create-on-brand-navigation-experiences-in-sap-build-apps-using-custom/ba-p/13617392

Essentially you could do 2 ways, both involve disabling the default navigation.

  • You can create a container the contains icons or text that are the navigation menu, and set visibility to true/false based on whatever you decide, for example, some page variable that tracks the user's role. We did this here in our challenge 2 years ago -- and you can even download it to see it: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-discussions/april-citizen-developer-challenge-sap-build-apps-task-0/m-p/13654312
  • You can use the marketplace component "Responsive navigation bar" with the custom navigation lists you can create (go to Settings > Navigation). Create a list for each role and then display that navigation based on the user's role. Essentially the same as the first but you hide/show an entire navigation bar. I don't like this one so much.

All of this is my understanding, but Mari will likely know more.

Enhancing Coding Experience with AI Assistance in Chrome DevTools

AI assistance in Chrome DevTools has multiple benefits that can significantly enhance your coding experience. 

Firstly, it provides intelligent code completion and suggestions, helping to reduce errors and increase productivity. With AI analyzing patterns in your code, it offers relevant suggestions and auto-complete code snippets to save you time and effort.

Additionally, AI-powered dev tools enable advanced debugging capabilities. By using machine learning algorithms, it identifies and highlights potential bugs, making it easier to locate and resolve issues in your code. This real-time error detection can save hours of solving time-consuming debugging.

Furthermore, AI-powered dev tools can assist in code updating, offering automated suggestions for optimizing and rearranging code to improve performance and readability. 

You just have to right-click on the element and click on “Ask AI”. 

Explanation starts providing on how to solve the error

Sources Tab

You just have to right-click on the element and click on “Ask AI”. 

Console Errors:

  • You can understand and solve the errors in your code. Just right-click on the error line and select understand this warning. 

AI Provides insights on how to fix the code with proper SAP UI5 Best Practices

Network Tab - Ask AI

Interact with Chat based conversation to find the network traces or traffic

Final Words

AI assistance in Chrome DevTools is a game-changer for web developers. By providing real-time insights, enhancing styling and layout understanding, optimizing performance, simplifying network request analysis, and enabling safe experimentation, it can significantly enhance your coding experience. Embrace this innovative tool and take your web development skills to the next level.

entry.title: Material determination in New Resource-Related Intercompany Billing-SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

Resource-related intercompany billing is a significant process for cross-company billing of intercompany services and expenses. The intercompany billing process in S/4HANA Public cloud is greatly simplified with introduction of margin with cost allocations and cost-plus billing. The underlying material determination plays a crucial role because the material is one of the significant attributes influencing the net rate with which the intercompany postings are billed.

In the new resource-related intercompany billing in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, the criteria based on which the material is determined can be flexibly maintained by customers via Self-Service Configuration User Interface (SSCUI). It is strongly recommended to maintain the configuration for material determination before the execution of the new resource-related intercompany billing process.

 This article details how the SSCUI - Maintain Billing Profile (ID 103637) can be configured for material determination.

The SSCUI for material determination

To allow a flexible configuration of material determination, the SSCUI- Maintain Billing Profile is introduced and can be maintained by customers. Also, when a billing material is not determined for an intercompany posting, this posting is excluded from the Intercompany Billing process. Hence, it is crucial that the material is correctly determined, which makes configuring the Billing Profile important.

Note: Though a posting will be excluded for the Intercompany Billing process if the billing material could not be determined, the rest of the process will continue, i.e rest of the postings will be checked for material determination and further billing process will be performed.  

The Billing Profile consists of three nodes.

  • Billing Profile
  • Billing Material Determination
  • Billing Material Determination Criteria

Note: The content in these nodes is not pre-delivered. It is mandatory for customers to configure it.

 

 

  • Billing Profile

A new entry needs to be created with ICOBLG001 as the Billing Profile. This is the profile that needs to be created for intercompany billing.

  • Billing Material Determination

Under the profile ICOBLG001, entries must be created for the materials that require determination.

 

Line: The order in which the criteria match check of the intercompany cost posting is done, is controlled by the line number maintained for the material. You can refer to the section Significance of line number in the Billing Material Determination node in this blog for additional information.

Material Direct: If an intercompany cost posting has Material, the material in the cost posting is identified as the Determined Material. You can refer to the section Direct Material in this blog for additional information.

Qty/Costs: Transfer Costs Only

As cost allocations support ICO margin and cost-plus billing is only supported in Intercompany Billing in S/4HANA Public cloud, ‘Transfer Costs Only’ option is the only option under ‘Qty/Costs’, for Billing Profile for Intercompany Billing.

With this, only cost in the intercompany posting is considered during the Net rate calculation. Quantity of the intercompany posting is not considered during the Net rate calculation.

Material: Billing material that needs to be determined

Srvc/Expn:

In the subsequent documents of a Billing Document Request, such as the Preliminary Billing Document or Billing Document, the preview displays the list of intercompany postings which are included in the document.

When the Srvc/Expn attribute is set as Service then the intercompany posting which has the corresponding material as the determined billing material will be classified as Service posting.

When the Srvc/Expn attribute is set as Expense then the intercompany posting which has the corresponding material as the determined billing material will be classified as Expense posting.

When the Srvc/Expn attribute is empty then the intercompany postings which have quantity are classified as Service postings and the rest are classified as Expense postings.

3) Billing Material Determination and Billing Material Determination Criteria:

The criteria on which the material determination is based, should be provided as entries under the node - Billing Material Determination Criteria.

The criteria can be based on the following three attributes.

  • Activity Type
  • G/L Account Number
  • Material Number

The attributes in the intercompany posting are checked against these criteria maintained in the Maintain Billing Profile SSCUI. When there is criteria match, the corresponding material that is maintained will be determined as the billing material.

For each of these three attributes, a ‘Set Name’ or a ‘Value’ can be maintained.

  • If a Value is maintained, it will be checked if the attribute of the intercompany posting matches the maintained value.
  • If a Set Name is maintained, it will be checked if the attribute of the intercompany posting belongs to the Set maintained in the Set Name.

 

 

Note: The G/L account number or the G/L account set should be part of the G/L account super set YBPS_BILLI. Only cost postings with a G/L account, belonging to the super set YBPS_BILLI, will be considered for intercompany billing.

Let’s have a look at an example.

Billing Material Determination:

Line

Material

100

T001

 

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -100

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T001

G/L Account Number

YBPS_ICO

 

Note: In this example, G/L accounts 94308000(Consulting) and 97777700 (ICO Margin) are maintained under the G/L account set YBPS_ICO. G/L account set YBPS_ICO is maintained under YBPS_BILLI.

 

Intercompany postings

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

1

T001

94308000

 

ICO Margin record for the posting 1

T001

97777700

 

2

T002

94308000

 

ICO Margin record for the posting 2

T002

97777700

 

 

In such a case, for posting 1 and its ICO margin posting, since the activity type and G/L account number match with the criteria maintained for line 100, the material in line 100, i.e., T001, will be the determined material.

For posting 2 and its ICO margin posting, since the activity type T002 does not match with the activity type maintained for line 100, the material will not be determined.

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

Determined Material

1

T001

94308000

 

T001

ICO Margin record for the posting 1

T001

97777700

 

T001

2

T002

94308000

 

Material not determined

ICO Margin record for the posting 2

T002

97777700

 

Material not determined

 

Significance of line number in the Billing Material Determination node

The matching criteria are sequentially checked against the criteria maintained for the materials in the Material Determination node, in ascending order. Once the criteria are matched, further processing stops, and the corresponding Material in the Material Determination node is identified as the Determined Material.

The order is controlled by the line number maintained in the Material Determination node.

Example:

Billing Material Determination:

Line

Material

100

T001

200

T999

300

T002

 

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -100

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T001

G/L Account Number

94308000

 

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -200

Field Name

Value

G/L Account Number

94308000

 

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -300

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T002

G/L Account Number

94308000

 

Intercompany postings

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

1

T001

94308000

 

2

T002

94308000

 

 

For posting 1, the attributes in the intercompany posting match with the criteria maintained for line-100. Hence T001 will be the determined material.

For posting 2, the attributes in the intercompany posting do not match with the criteria maintained for line-100 as the activity type does not match in this case. Therefore, the attributes are checked for the criteria maintained in the next line, line 200. The G/L account in the intercompany posting matches with the G/L account maintained in the criteria for line 200, and this is the only criteria for line 200. Hence the corresponding Material, T999, will be the determined material.

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

Determined Material

1

T001

94308000

 

T001

2

T002

94308000

 

T999

 

However, if the expectation is that T002 should be determined, then the order of the entries in Billing Material Determination node, should be changed.

Billing Material Determination:

Line

Material

100

T001

200

T002

300

T999

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -100

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T001

G/L Account Number

94308000

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -200

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T002

G/L Account Number

94308000

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -300

Field Name

Value

G/L Account Number

94308000

 

The G/L account and activity type in the intercompany posting match the G/L account and Activity Type maintained in the criteria for line 200. Hence, its corresponding material T002 will be the determined material.

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

Determined Material

1

T001

94308000

 

T001

2

T002

94308000

 

T002

 

Recommendation: It is recommended to maintain specific criteria for the top materials in the Material Determination node and maintain generic criteria for the bottom materials.

Direct material

For the entries in the Material Determination node, the ‘Material Direct’ attribute can be set.

When this attribute is set, if the corresponding criteria matches, and if the cost posting has Material, the material in the cost posting is identified as the Determined Material. However, if the criteria matches but there is no material in the cost posting, then the material in the corresponding Material Determination node will be identified as the Determined Material.

Billing Material Determination:

Line

Mat. dir.

Material

400

True

Z001

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -400

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T005

G/L Account Number

94308000

 

Cost postings

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

1

T005

94308000

Mat1

2

T005

94308000

 

For posting 1, there is criteria match, and the cost posting has Mat1 as the material. Hence Mat1 is identified as Determined Material.

For posting 2, the criteria matches but the cost posting does not have material. Hence Z001, the material in line 400, is identified as Determined Material.

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

Determined Material

1

T005

94308000

Mat1

Mat1

2

T005

94308000

 

Z001

 

Fallback option

If there is an entry in the Material Determination node for which no criterion is maintained, then the corresponding material in this line will be identified as the Determined Material when none of the criteria match.

Billing Material Determination:

Line

Material

100

T001

200

T002

300

T999

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -100

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T001

G/L Account Number

94308000

Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -200

Field Name

Value

Activity Type

T002

G/L Account Number

94308000

 

No Billing Material Determination Criteria for Material Determination line -300

Cost postings

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

1

T001

94308000

 

2

T002

94308000

 

3

T003

94308000

 

For the posting 3 as none of the criteria matches and for Line-300 as no criteria is maintained, T999 will be Determined Material.

Posting

Activity Type

G/L Account Number

Material

Determined Material

1

T001

94308000

 

T001

2

T002

94308000

 

T002

3

T003

94308000

 

T999

 

Note: In the above example if the line 300 with material T999 is not maintained at all, then the posting 3 will not be considered for the intercompany billing and error will be raised. However, the intercompany billing process will happen considering postings 1 & 2.

Summary

Maintaining the Billing Profile configuration helps in flexibly setting the criteria based on which the materials can be determined. This is a mandatory configuration that needs to be maintained so that the right material can be identified for the intercompany postings to be accounted for in the intercompany billing process.

entry.title: Vorstellung der SAP Academic Board DACH Arbeitsgruppe: "Technologie"
entry.content:
Sehr geehrte akademische Community in Deutschland, Österreich und der Schweiz,

nach der Vorstellung der DACH Academic Board Arbeitsgruppe “Lehrmethoden” möchte ich Ihnen nun die Arbeitsgruppe “Technologie”, die von Prof. Dr. Stefan Stöckler, Prof. Sebastien Gard und Prof. Dr. Sebastian Baltes geleitet wird, vorstellen. Die drei Experten haben wir zu ihrer Arbeit, Motivation und Plänen für die Arbeitsgruppe befragt.

Viel Spass beim Lesen und gehen Sie gerne bei Nachfragen direkt auf die drei Kollegen zu.

André Biener (Director, SAP University Alliances)

****
André Biener (AB): “Hallo Stefan, Sebastien und Sebastian. Könnt Ihr Euch bitte kurz vorstellen und erklären, welche spezifischen Ziele Ihr in Eurer Arbeitsgruppe im DACH Academic Board verfolgt?”

Prof. Dr. Stefan Stöckler (SS):   “Gemeinsam mit mir arbeiten im Kern-Team dieses Bereichs noch
Sébastien Gard von der HES-SO Valais-Wallis, Hochschule für angewandte Wissenschaften und Kunst (Schweiz) und Sebastian Baltes von der Universität Bayreuth (Deutschland).

Wichtig ist uns, die vorhandenen Technologien über die gesamte Automatisierungspyramide, also von IoT bis zum ERP-System, in unterschiedlichen Kombinationen und vor allem im praktischen Einsatz zu zeigen und zu lehren. An der Ostschweizer Fachhochschule haben wir z.B. eine Smart Factory aufgebaut, die ein reales Abbild eines klassischen Unternehmens mit mehreren Standorten und Produktionslinien ist. Ein weiterer Ausbau in Verbindung mit innovativen Technologien sowie laufender Beobachtung von sehr differenzierten Nachhaltigkeitsparametern ist geplant und soll unsere Studierenden fit für die künftige Praxis in einer VUCA-Welt machen.”


Prof. Dr. Stefan Stöckler

Prof. Sebastien Gard (SG): “Ein Hauptziel unserer Arbeitsgruppe besteht darin, der  SAP University Alliances Community hervorragendes Lehrmaterial, Anwendungsfälle, Lehrpläne und Zugang zu neuen SAP-Produkten bereitzustellen, um Dozenten bei ihrer Arbeit zu unterstützen. Im Gegensatz zu anderen Arbeitsgruppen, die sich mehr auf die geschäftlichen Aspekte konzentrieren, möchten wir tiefer auf die technischen Aspekte verschiedener Produkte und Technologien eingehen. ”

Prof. Dr. Sebastian Baltes (SB): “Eine kritische Stimme zu sein, die den Einsatz von SAP-Technologien in der Softwareentwicklung exploriert, aber auch klar auf Lücken und Unzulänglichkeiten hinweist. Mir persönlich wäre es auch wichtig neben der Lehre das Thema Forschung im SAP Academic Board stärker zu vertreten. ”

AB: “Eure Arbeitsgruppe konzentriert sich auf Technologie. Was ist Eurer Meinung nach der wichtigste Schwerpunkt in diesem Bereich und warum?”

SS: “Wir sehen den Schwerpunkt dieses Themas in zwei Richtungen. Einerseits kommen immer mehr neue Technologien rund um das ERP-Kernsystem zum Einsatz. Darauf hat ja SAP mit der Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) schon weitsichtig reagiert. D.h. es ist wichtig, dass Themen wie No/Low-Code ebenso wie Pro-Code Programmierung, künstliche Intelligenz, Data Analytics oder Systemintegration adressiert werden. Sie stellen unter anderem eine wichtige Basis für die Digitale Transformation hin zu Smart Factorys dar. Andererseits wollen wir genau in diesem Thema, Smart Factorys und Industrie 4.0, das Querschnittsthema „Nachhaltigkeitsmanagement“ verankern und stärken. Dazu ist ein umfassendes Wissen um die Möglichkeiten, Stärken und Schwächen der einzelnen Technologien notwendig. ”

SG: “Heute spricht jeder über KI (insbesondere LLMs), daher ist dies offensichtlich ein wichtiger Schwerpunkt. Es besteht jedoch ein erheblicher Unterschied zwischen dem Erstellen einer KI, dem Anwenden von ML-Algorithmen oder dem bloßen Einbetten bestehender KI-Technologie in ein Produkt.

In Anbetracht der Zahl der Studierenden, die mit SAP-Produkten in Berührung kommen, ist die Verwendung der Technologie relevanter. Daher ist es sinnvoller, unsere Bemühungen auf SAP BTP zu konzentrieren, da dies die Verwendung einer Vielzahl neuer Technologien, Dienste und Produkte in den verschiedenen Lehrveranstaltungen ermöglicht.”

Prof. Sebastien Gard

SB: “Die Arbeitsgruppe „Technologie“ deckt unterschiedliche Aspekte ab. Mein persönlicher Schwerpunkt ist die Softwareentwicklung in der Cloud. Mit der Business Technology Platform (BTP) hat die SAP in den vergangenen Jahren gute Fortschritte in diesem Bereich gemacht, aber gleichzeitig sehe ich auch ungenutzte Potentiale im Bereich Automatisierung; „ClickOps“ ist leider noch recht verbreitet. Auch das Thema Developer Experience ist mir wichtig. Hier stellt das SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) interessante Konzepte bereit. Ich freue mich, dass die SAP schon lange im Bereich Open-Source aktiv ist und hoffe, dass zukünftig auch CAP als Open-Source-Projekt verfügbar sein wird. Dies wäre für mich eine Voraussetzung für den Einsatz in der Lehre. ”

AB: “Welche Technologien wie Künstliche Intelligenz oder Automatisierung nutzt Ihr in Euren Forschungsprojekten, um Geschäftsprozesse zu optimieren?”

SS: “In der aktuellen Forschung verbinden wir Technologien wie Process Mining, Process Simulation mit KI- und Data-Analytics-Methoden, um ein prozess- und datenbasiertes Nachhaltigkeitsmanagement zu etablieren. Daher kommen um diesen Kern auch weitere Technologien zum Einsatz, um die notwendigen Basisdaten aus den unterschiedlichen System-Ebenen zu ermitteln. ”

SG:“Ich persönlich verwende in meiner täglichen Arbeit robotergestützte Prozessautomatisierung (und heutzutage „intelligente“ RPA) sowie natürlich KI-Assistenten. Die Produktivitätssteigerung durch diese Tools ist ziemlich beeindruckend.”

SB: “Mein Bereich ist Software Engineering, daher optimiere ich in meiner Forschung primär Softwareentwicklungsprozesse. Hier gibt es noch große Potentiale zur Automatisierung – und das ganz ohne künstliche Intelligenz. Aber natürlich evaluieren wir in meiner Gruppe auch die Potentiale von künstlicher Intelligenz, allerdings immer mit einem breiten Blick auf die Entwicklungsprozesse. Sprich: Wir evaluieren nicht nur die Generierung von Quellcode, sondern auch frühere Prozessschritte wie das Requirements Engineering.”

Prof. Dr. Sebastian Baltes

AB: “Gibt es aktuelle Projekte oder Studien in Eurer Arbeitsgruppe, die besonders bahnbrechend oder innovativ sind?”

SS: “Ich denke der hier geschilderte Ansatz von einem holistischen, system-immanenten und laufenden Nachhaltigkeitsmanagement ist durchaus innovativ und stellt eine klare Trendwende vom – oft als sehr aufwändig beschriebenen – CSR-Reporting hin zu einem ESG-Controlling dar. Diese Themen werden jedoch aktuell nicht in der Arbeitsgruppe, sondern an der Ostschweizer Fachhochschule gemeinsam mit der Hochschule Harz, vertreten durch den Kollegen Hans-Jürgen Scheruhn, der die Arbeitsgruppe „Business Process Management“ leitet, vorangetrieben.”

SG:  “Die von Stefan entwickelte SmartFactory ist ein hervorragendes Beispiel für ein komplexes Projekt, das viele verschiedene Spitzentechnologien im Bereich Industrie 4.0 umfasst und integriert. ”

SB: “Mein Doktorvater hat immer gesagt: „Nur Hühner gackern über ungelegte Eier.“ Daher möchte ich weniger über unsere laufenden Projekte sprechen als über die bereits publizierten Arbeiten. Hier würde ich insbesondere unsere Forschung in Zusammenarbeit mit Kollegen von SAP HANA hervorheben. Wir haben damit zu seinem besseren Verständnis von möglichen Ineffizienzen bei der Ausführung von Testfällen beigetragen und es geschafft, sowohl den Entwicklungsprozess von SAP HANA ein kleines Stück zu verbessern als auch die Ergebnisse akademisch gut zu publizieren. ”

AB: “Wie integriert Ihr die Erkenntnisse aus Eurer Forschung in die Lehrmethoden, und wie profitieren die Studierenden davon?”

SS: “Die Forschungsergebnisse werden so schnell wie möglich auch in der SmartFactory@OST nachgestellt, damit die Studierenden dort ein State-of-the-Art vorfinden und anhand dieser realen Umgebung geschult werden können. Hier sind wir noch sehr auf die Unterstützung von SAP angewiesen, da viele der neuen Technologien nur bedingt für die Hochschulen zugänglich sind und vor allem noch nicht in spezifischen, individuellen Umgebungen genutzt werden können. Das Lehrmaterial macht die Themen jedoch nur dann „begreifbar“, wenn anhand bestehender Maschinen und Produkten sowie spezifischen Prozessen und Daten gelernt und getestet werden kann.”

SG: “Wir versuchen immer, unseren Unterricht in der Praxis zu verankern. Alle aktuellen Projekte sowie die guten und schlechten Erfahrungen aus der Forschung sind daher eine hervorragende Inspirationsquelle und ein hervorragendes Material für die Vorbereitung der verschiedenen Vorlesungen. Wir versuchen auch immer, diese Beispiele in das Lehrmaterial einzubringen, das wir der UA-Community zur Verfügung stellen.”

SB: “In meinem Forschungsfeld Empirical Software Engineering gibt es beispielsweise die Möglichkeit, dass Studierende selbst kleine Studien mit Open-Source-Projekten durchführen, die sie dann im Rahmen einer Abschlussarbeit vertiefen können. Wir haben es schon häufiger geschafft, dass Studierende nach der Abgabe ihrer Abschlussarbeiten so erste Erfahrung im akademischen Publizieren sammeln konnten. In meinen Vorlesungen versuche ich auch immer den aktuellen Forschungsstand zusammenzufassen und auf aktuelle Forschungsergebnisse zu verweisen. ”

AB: “Welche Rolle spielt das Thema Technologie in der interdisziplinären Lehre und Forschung an Euren akademischen Einrichtungen?”

SS: “Das ist eine interessante Frage, die genau auf die Kernidee unserer SmartFactory@OST abzielt. Seit über drei Jahren bauen wir diese Lern- und künftige Forschungsumgebung auf, um Studierende aus unterschiedlichen Fachbereichen am selben Objekt zu lehren. So können Betriebsökonominnen und -ökonomen z.B. die Produkt- oder Produktionskosten für unsere real existierenden Artikel berechnen, während die Wirtschaftsinformatikerinnen und -informatiker teilweise gemeinsam mit dem Bereich Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen die Prozesse definieren und die Systeme konfigurieren und integrieren. Mechatronik, Werkstoffkunde, Maschinenbau und Produktionstechnik sind weitere Fachbereiche, die von dieser Anlage profitieren.”

SG: “Da meine Universität im Bereich Wirtschaft angesiedelt ist, wird die Technologie als Mittel zum Erreichen bestimmter Ziele betrachtet und nicht als Selbstzweck. Per Definition müssen die Studierenden verstehen, wie sie die Technologie in ihrer eigenen Disziplin und Forschung nutzen und anwenden können."

SB: “Eine sehr große! Die Universität Bayreuth setzt stark auf Interdisziplinarität, insbesondere in der angewandten KI-Forschung. Auch im Software Engineering argumentiere ich schon lange, dass die wirklich interessanten und relevanten Probleme oft nur interdisziplinär gelöst werden können. ”

AB: “Was sind die größten Herausforderungen im Bereich Technologien in den nächsten fünf Jahren, und wie plant Eure Arbeitsgruppe, diese zu bewältigen?”

SS: “Eine wesentliche Herausforderung ist sicher der schnelle technologische Wandel, der ins Lehr- und Forschungsumfeld einbezogen werden muss. Eine engere Kooperation mit internen Entwicklungsgruppen und Expertinnen und Experten der SAP wäre hier sicher ein großer Gewinn, um Hochschulen und Universitäten auf dem aktuellen Stand zu halten. Der Aufwand für die Wissenserschließung und anschließende Umsetzung in Lehrmaterial wir oft unterschätzt. ”

SG: “Die Technologie verbessert und verändert sich heute extrem schnell. Die größte Herausforderung besteht darin, herauszufinden, welche Technologie in 5 Jahren noch relevant sein wird. Der Grund dafür ist, dass zwischen dem Zeitpunkt, an dem wir uns für eine Technologie entscheiden, das neue Lehrmaterial vorbereiten, es an den verschiedenen Universitäten verbreiten und die Studenten ihre Abschlüsse machen, eine Technologie bereits veraltet oder irrelevant sein kann.  An diesem Punkt müssen wir uns auch auf die von SAP selbst bereitgestellten Leitlinien verlassen, um herauszufinden, welche Technologie für den Unterricht sinnvoll ist.”

SB: “Die größte Herausforderung wird es sein, nach dem initialen GenAI-Hype die vielversprechenden von den unrealistischen Anwendungsfällen zu trennen und darauf hinzuarbeiten, mögliche Produktivitätsgewinne wissenschaftlich fundiert zu quantifizieren. ”

AB: “Welche Rolle spielen Kooperationen mit Unternehmen oder anderen Universitäten in Eurer Arbeit?”

SS: “Als Fachhochschule bieten wir Dienstleistungen an die Unternehmen in unserem Einzugsbereich, das ist eine großartige Möglichkeit einerseits die aktuelle Praxis zu erleben und andererseits innovative Lösungen in die Firmen zu bringen. Natürlich spielen spezielle Unternehmen auch eine große Rolle als Sponsoren für unsere SmartFactory@OST. So sind die Produktionsmaschinen für eine Hochschule nicht leistbar, wenn nicht großzügige Hersteller Unterstützung leisten. ”

SG:  “Unternehmen sind eine unerschöpfliche Quelle für Anwendungsfälle und Präsentationen für die verschiedenen Vorlesungen. Studenten sind immer interessiert, wenn wir eine externe Person einladen, ein aktuelles, reales Projekt vorzustellen. Außerdem werden die meisten Bachelorarbeitsthemen in Zusammenarbeit mit Unternehmen erstellt. ”

SB: “Diese Kooperationen sind enorm wichtig und wertvoll. In fast allen Forschungsprojekten arbeiten wir mit Kolleginnen und Kollegen von anderen Universitäten oder aus Unternehmen zusammen. ”

AB: “Welche zukünftigen Projekte oder Initiativen planen Ihr in Eurer Arbeitsgruppe?”

SS: “Derzeit arbeiten wir vor allem daran, die Lehrumgebungen in den unterschiedlichen Implementierungen der einzelnen Hochschulen verfügbar zu machen. Die notwendige Systemintegrationen und die verschiedenen Lehrinhalte erfordern sehr spezifische Umgebungen. Dies muss entwickelt und der laufende Betrieb mittels Support abgesichert werden. Hier braucht es noch neue Strukturen und Lösungen. ”

SG:  “Im Moment führe ich ein Pilotprojekt für einen neuen Lehrplan zur SAP BTP Integration Suite durch. Ein weiterer Bereich, der mich interessiert und für den Bedarf besteht, sind Low-Code/No-Code-Plattformen wie Build on BTP oder Mendix. ”

SB: “Mir wäre es ein Anliegen, Open Source und Cloud Native Softwareentwicklung noch stärker in den Fokus des SAP-Ökosystem zu rücken. ”

entry.title: GL accounts included in EC01 copy company code transport request
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a situation where while copying a company code from existing company code, the GL accounts were also copied in to transport request from Development system and the Transport request is already moved to quality system. (Chart of accounts is same for both existing and new company code )

Now, we want to create a new transport request to delete the object SKB1 from the original transport request. So that GL accounts are not copied from Development system to production system. Because there is inconsistent data in Development and Production system.

Is there any way to achive this.? Please let me know 

System is SAP ECC 6.0

Thank you,

Sumant

entry.title: Flexible Column Layout in SAP UI5
entry.content:
Is there a way to manually change the values for layout data for desktop or tablet. In thinkpad laptops, the Device API detects the device as both desktop and tablet hence providing only 33 and 67% ratios in the below provided layout, though I want it to be 25/50/25 as provided for desktop. 

I even tried using this approach, but doesn't seem to work. 

    <App>
        <pages>
            <f:FlexibleColumnLayout
                id="flexibleColumnLayout"
                layout="ThreeColumnsMidExpanded"
            >
                <f:layoutData>
                    <f:FlexibleColumnLayoutData>
                        <f:desktopLayoutData>
                            <f:FlexibleColumnLayoutDataForDesktop
                                threeColumnsMidExpanded="20/60/20"
                            />
                        </f:desktopLayoutData>
                        <f:tabletLayoutData>
                            <f:FlexibleColumnLayoutDataForTablet
                                threeColumnsMidExpanded="20/60/20">
                               
                            </f:FlexibleColumnLayoutDataForTablet>
                        </f:tabletLayoutData>
                    </f:FlexibleColumnLayoutData>
                </f:layoutData>
                <f:beginColumnPages>
                    <Page showHeader="false">
                        <content>
                            <List
                                id="userTrainingPlansList"
                                items="{userTrainingPlans>/}"
                                headerText="{i18n>existingPlans}"
                            >
                                <items>
                                    <StandardListItem
                                        title="{userTrainingPlans>TrainingPlanName}"
                                    />
                                </items>
                            </List>
                        </content>
                    </Page>
                </f:beginColumnPages>
                <f:midColumnPages />
                <f:endColumnPages />
            </f:FlexibleColumnLayout>
        </pages>
    </App>

Kindly provide a solution for this if possible.

entry.title: SAP Build Code Joule Evolves: Unveiling Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation
entry.content:
As of early 2025, the optimal approach for professional developers working with Generative AI involves creating project skeletons that cover general requirements, followed by incremental enhancements. SAP Build Code Joule expands on this practice with multi-agent support, allowing developers to use a single prompt to engage specialized agents for various tasks. By coordinating these agents, Joule creates more comprehensive project foundations, thereby streamlining the development process. This approach enables developers to focus more on refining and customizing their applications, improving productivity and efficiency.

To align with these best practices for Build Code CAP developers, we've begun supporting more complex requirements for initial CAP application generation.

Note: If you're more used to the previous data model generation process, you can use a newly introduced command specifically for this purpose: /cap-gen-model.

Let's walk through the end-to-end flow to see these improvements in detail.

 

1. Create Project

Create a new Build Code Full-Stack Application from SAP Build Lobby via this navigation path: Create -> Build an Application -> SAP Build Code -> Full-Stack Application.

Note: Choose the 'Node' item from the 'Development Stack' dropdown box to see the new improvements.

Once the status of the newly created application changes from 'Pending Creation' to a normal status, click the application name to load it into the Build Code development environment. By default, the Storyboard will open automatically, and you can access the Build Code Joule panel from the left sidebar.

 

2. Initialize CAP Application

Select the /cap-gen-app command. You'll notice a new example prompt added for the capex application. You can view the full content in the Joule input field once it's selected.

Note:

For backward compatibility, SAP Build Code Joule maintains the previous functionality alongside the new multi-agent approach. You can still use the simpler generation process by typing the /cap-gen-app command without a detailed prompt, which triggers the original single-agent generation. This short-term solution allows you to choose between a comprehensive multi-agent approach for complex applications and a quicker, simpler generation for basic needs. Keep in mind that the richness of your input directly influences the comprehensiveness of the generated output.

Now, instead of showing the generated result for a simple request (only data model-related), an execution plan is displayed. The complex requirements are broken down into subtasks and we apply our best practices. As an end-user, you can review the improved user requirements and enhance them manually (via the pencil action button). This follows a popular human-in-the-loop pattern for working with Generative AI. We believe it's always easier for users to improve requirements using human language rather than adjusting generated code, which may be less familiar.

Once the improved requirements in steps are satisfactory, you can trigger the generation step-by-step manually.

Let's start with step 1 to generate the CAP data model.

After executing step 1, step 2 becomes available. You can check the generated content in the bottom 'Staging Files' UI block. Click any item to open the file content in Build Code.

Note:

  1. The staging files are in a temporary folder for review purposes only. You can manually change them if you're certain there won't be unexpected consequences for the following steps.
  2. You can rerun a step if you encounter any failures or are unsatisfied with the existing content.

Before running step 2 for sample data, let's improve it further to make the sample data more specific. Edit it, save it, and run it.

Then, proceed to step 3 for custom application logic.

You'll see two new files generated per the requirement for this step:

  1. The 'service.js' file: mapping configuration for the application logic handler and service entity events.
  2. The 'before-asserts-create.js' file: new logic attached to the entity 'Asserts' create event, triggered before every assert entity creation.

Background info on how Build Code Joule determines the application logic configuration:

  1. Validation checking logic: typically attached to the entity 'CREATE' or 'UPDATE' event in the 'before' phase.
  2. Entity property value calculation logic: typically attached to the entity 'READ' event in the 'after' phase.
  3. Custom service logic for a CAP action or function: no standard entity event, typically attached to the 'on' phase.

Note:

  1. Describe the app logic requirement more specifically to align with the above guidelines.
  2. You can still change the configuration later (after accepting the staging files) using the CAP application logic editor and adjust the logic content using the Build Code Joule command /cap-app-logic.

When all steps are executed successfully, the 'Accept' button becomes visible under the staging files. Accept the content into your working project if you're satisfied with the staging content.

The Storyboard will refresh to reflect all updated aspects: data model, sample data, service entities, and the application logic attached to the service entity.

You can switch to the detail view of the Storyboard for a better overview of each entity.

 

3. Preview the Application

After initializing the application according to the business requirements, it's time to preview it. Click the green triangle button in the top-right corner to launch it.

The CAP application will launch in local mode with sample data in a new window.

Use the 'View as table' button to check the runtime content for each entity, e.g., the 'Asserts' entity.

Click the 'Create' button to verify the application logic and see if there's a warning when the cost is exceeded. Test with sample input values.

You should see a warning message for a negative use case test and successful creation for a positive use case test.

If the application logic runs as expected, great! However, you may sometimes need to troubleshoot unexpected code issues (including data model, sample data, and FE UI annotations), debug, and ask Build Code Joule to enhance the code or make manual changes.

A Useful Tip: To improve logic codes using Build Code Joule with additional runtime data structure as the reference.

Additional info: In this flow, the cost 'Currency' field is not covered. If you click the value help button beside the cost value, you may find it empty.

Try solving this via manual changes or using Data Editor & Build Code Joule to enhance it. Here's a sample content for reference (/db/data/sap.common-Currencies.csv)

 

name;descr;code;symbol;minorUnit
US Dollar;United States Dollar;USD;$;2
Euro;Euro Member Countries;EUR;€;2
Japanese Yen;Japan Yen;JPY;¥;0
British Pound;United Kingdom Pound;GBP;£;2
Australian Dollar;Australia Dollar;AUD;$;2

 

Once everything looks good, return to Build Code and stop the preview.

As mentioned in the beginning, working with Generative AI on top of initially generated artifacts from Build Code Joule provides a good starting point for further improvements to meet additional requirements. Use Build Code Joule via available commands or visual productivity tools for further changes.

 

4. Deploy the Application

Finally, when the project is ready for deployment, navigate to the Task Explorer to deploy this app to BTP.

You'll be able to access this new CAP service app using a new BTP deployed URL.

Note:

  1. There's no 'View as table' action button for the deployed application, as it's only for local development purposes.
  2. You need create FE (or MDK) UI applications for the service entities separately.

 

Summary

We hope you now have a better understanding of our latest improvements to CAP application initial generation and how it can streamline your development work. In the future, we plan to extend similar support to additional generation scenarios.

Enjoy coding with Build Code Joule assistant!

entry.title: Restart specific step in flexible workflow for purchase requisitions
entry.content:
• Current Logic: In the current workflow, if an approver at Level 10 rejects a PR, the entire workflow restarts from the beginning.
  • New Requirement: Management requires the ability for an approver (e.g., Level 10) to reject the PR and have the workflow restart at a specific prior level (e.g., Level 5) rather than restarting the entire process from the beginning.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restart specific step in flexible workflow for purcha...
entry.content:
Hello customer, 

In the standard process, you can only define whether the current step should restart or not after rejection. In the Exception Handling section of the Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions app, the options available for 'Action Result' are:

  • Cancel workflow - the entire workflow will be cancelled
  • Continue – the approval process goes to the next level, irrespective of the decision at that level
  • Repeat step – the same step is triggered again
  • Restart workflow – it re-triggers the workflow from the beginning

You can also implement custom conditions to trigger specific steps based on your needs using the BAdIs mentioned in KBA 2767845.

Best regards, 
Tatiana

entry.title: 基于按单生产和可配置产品的高级公司间销售
entry.content:
前提条件

使用7DM-基于按单生产和可配置产品的高级公司间销售，需要先激活以下业务场景：

1YT – Make-to-Order Production with Variant Configuration

BJE – Make-to-Order Production - Finished Goods Sales and Final Assembly

5D2 – Advanced Intercompany Sales

同时需要完成5D2和7DM相关Set-up instructions中所有的配置

客户需要通过创建事件申请Expert Configuration来激活高级公司间销售，可以先在测试系统申请并测试，如果发现细节部分无法满足客户需求也可以再次通过事件申请Expert Configuration来移除高级公司间销售的配置，从而可以继续使用经典公司间销售。

高级公司间销售与经典公司间销售无法同时并存。

流程介绍

从流程上对比经典公司间销售，高级公司间销售会产生公司间销售订单与公司间采购订单并且对应公司间销售发票和公司间供应商发票，有个细节是经典公司间销售流程中公司间销售发票是需要在创建完客户发票后才能生成的，而高级公司间销售并没有这个限制。同时高级公司间销售使用了在途功能。

系统实现

1.创建客户销售订单

业务场景：7DM不仅支持MTO并且支持可配置物料AVC：

2.通过APP：监控价值链，可以看到公司间销售订单和公司间采购订单由系统自动创建：

注意：公司间销售订单与公司间采购订单的价格分别需要通过condition record和purchase info record维护。目前系统无法产生关联创建。

思考：实际业务会有公司间销售订单与公司间采购订单两者价格不同的情况吗...？

3.进行MTO生产流程，请参考SI:1YT或者SI:BJE完成以下步骤

3.1 运行MRP （Material Requirements Planning for Sales Order）

3.2 创建生产订单（Create Production Order）

3.3 发布生产订单（Release Production Orders）

Area of Responsibility Production Supervisor: plant 1010; MTO VC -Valuated YB3

3.4 拣配组件（Pick Components）

3.5 确认组装活动（Confirm Assembly Activities）

对Operation 0010, 0031, 0032进行Post and Complete.

3.6 生产订单收货（Post good receipt for Production Order）

4.创建外向交货单5.拣配外向交货单和发货过账

发货过账前请检查内部和外部计划时间，计划时间设置在系统实际日期后，比如系统实际日期为2025.01.14，计划时间设定在2025.01.23

发货过账后查看产品库存状态，对应流程图中的步骤7：转移到工厂1010在途

6.确认实际转让日期

现在系统时间是2025.01.14，外向交货单内部转移计划时间是2025.01.23（在未来），此时我输入内部实际转移时间早于系统时间并且保存后：

系统自动生成以下物料凭证，对应流程图步骤8

查看此时库存状态，可以发现产品从工厂1010在途转移到工厂1710在途

根据实际业务情况，在外部转移确认时间字段输入时间后（早于系统时间），如下：

产品从工厂1710在途发货给了客户，对应流程图步骤9

注意：由于此步骤是在交货单PGI之后，所以在途库存没办法再进行拆分，比如需求：产品在工厂1010在途2件，请求先发1件到客户，这个是无法被满足的。

7.创建公司间销售发票IV2

系统会自动创建公司间供应商发票，APP：监控价值链同时会监控报错

8.创建客户销售发票F2

相关Blog

经典公司间销售:

基于MTO产品的公司间销售退货流程介绍 - SAP Community

基于MTO产品的公司间销售流程介绍 - SAP Community

感谢观看

entry.title: Advance Filter not working for contract end date field
entry.content:
We created some contracts with contract start date 06/01/DIGITS and end date 05/31/DIGITS. However, when using these 2 fields in the Advanced Filter to find the contracts, nothing is returned.

If I only use Contract Start Date, then I see the contracts result returned.

Can you tell why the contracts are not found out by the search?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Advance Filter not working for contract end date field
entry.content:
When a single date is set for the search field Contract End, the query itself takes a date range as input. 

For example, contract end is 01.01.2024, then the query takes the range between 2024-01-01T23:00:00Z and 2024-01-02T22:59:59Z.

In you case, the contract end date is in time zone PST. It converted into UTC+0 and saved in the backend. So the contract end date value is 2026-05-31T11:00:00.0000000Z which is out of the search time range and hence the contracts are not found out.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Agent Lifecycle Management
entry.content:
Hi AnjaniAL,

SAP SuccessFactors Agent Lifecycle Management --> Formerly  known as ProducerPro

SAP SuccessFactors Agent Performance Management --> Formerly  known as ProducerPro Enterprise(ICM)

SAP SuccessFactors Agent Performance Management has below features:

Core Features

  1. Rule-Based Payout Management
    • Flexible configuration of payout rules based on KPIs, business goals, and roles.
    • Support for fixed, variable, and tiered commission structures.
  2. Dynamic Incentive Modeling
    • Real-time updates to payout structures to align with changing business objectives.
    • "What-if" scenario analysis to forecast the impact of payout changes.
  3. Quota and Target Management
    • Ability to assign, monitor, and adjust quotas at individual, team, or organizational levels.
    • Integration with historical performance data to set realistic targets.
  4. Real-Time Analytics and Reporting
    • Dashboards for tracking payout trends, profitability, and performance metrics.
    • Drill-down capabilities to analyze individual or team-level performance.
  5. Integration with Enterprise Systems
    • Seamless integration with CRM, ERP, and HRMS platforms for data synchronization.
    • APIs for custom integrations and third-party tool compatibility.

Advanced Features

  1. Compliance and Audit Trails
    • Automated compliance checks to adhere to legal and policy standards.
    • Detailed audit logs for transparency and accountability.
  2. Customizable Payment Schedules
    • Options to support monthly, quarterly, or on-demand payouts.
    • Flexible timelines to accommodate regional or contractual needs.
  3. Dispute Resolution Mechanisms
    • Built-in workflows to handle payout disputes efficiently.
    • Automated validation of claims to reduce manual errors.
  4. Localization and Multi-Currency Support
    • Adaptation to local tax laws, currencies, and compliance requirements.
    • Support for global teams with multilingual interfaces.
  5. Retroactivity
  6. Hold functionality

 

User Experience Features

  1. Intuitive Interface
    • User-friendly dashboards for administrators, managers, and employees.
    • Mobile access for real-time payout insights on the go.
  2. Employee Self-Service Portal
    • Access to detailed payout breakdowns and performance benchmarks.
    • Tools for employees to track their progress toward earning incentives.
  3. Gamification Elements
    • Leaderboards and badges to encourage healthy competition.
    • Real-time notifications on achievements and payouts.

 

 

SAP SuccessFactors Agent Lifecycle Management has below Features:

Core Features

  1. Dynamic Incentive Modeling
    • Real-time updates to payout structures to align with changing business objectives.
    • "What-if" scenario analysis to forecast the impact of other integrated Payout systems payout changes.
  2. Real-Time Analytics and Reporting
    • Dashboards for tracking payout trends, profitability, and performance metrics.
    • Drill-down capabilities to analyze individual or team-level performance.
  3. Integration with Enterprise Systems
    • Seamless integration with CRM, ERP, and HRMS platforms for data synchronization.
    • APIs for custom integrations and third-party tool compatibility.

Advanced Features

  1. Compliance and Audit Trails
    • Automated compliance checks to adhere to legal and policy standards.
    • Detailed audit logs for transparency and accountability.
  2. Localization and Multi-Currency Support
    • Adaptation to local tax laws, currencies, and compliance requirements.
    • Support for global teams with multilingual interfaces.

User Experience Features

  1. Intuitive Interface
    • User-friendly dashboards for administrators, managers, and employees.
    • Mobile access for real-time payout insights on the go.
  2. Employee Self-Service Portal
    • Access to detailed payout breakdowns and performance benchmarks.
    • Tools for employees to track their progress toward earning incentives.
  3. Gamification Elements
    • Leaderboards and badges to encourage healthy competition.
    • Real-time notifications on achievements and payouts.
